title: Shopping
description: Browse, ask prices, try things on, and pay with confidence in Japanese shops.
situation: Buying things: prices, sizes, colours, asking to look around, and finishing the transaction politely.

phrases.tip: You don't reply to this; it is the greeting staff call out to every customer.

phrases.native: 試着してもいいですか
phrases.transliteration: shichaku shite mo ii desu ka
phrases.pronunciation: shee-chah-koo shteh moh ee dess kah

phrases.native: もっと大きいサイズはありますか
phrases.transliteration: motto ookii saizu wa arimasu ka
phrases.english: Do you have a bigger size?
phrases.pronunciation: moh-toh oh-kee sai-zoo wah ah-ree-mahs kah
phrases.tip: Swap 大きい (ookii, big) for 小さい (chiisai, small) when you need smaller.

phrases.transliteration: betsu no iro wa arimasu ka
phrases.pronunciation: beh-tsoo noh ee-roh wah ah-ree-mahs kah

phrases.tip: Many shops charge a few yen for bags now, so they often ask first.
Bring your passport; tourists can claim tax-free on many purchases.
